itemSimulatorWorkspace.title: Path of Exile 2 — Item simulator
itemSimulatorWorkspace.intro: Pick a base and target modifiers, then view a recommended crafting order that favours lower exalted-orb spend—the same cost axis used for reinforcement-learning rewards.

baseItem.title: Base item
baseItem.description: Select the item you are crafting on.

desiredMods.title: Modifiers
desiredMods.description: Add the modifiers you want on your item and adjust roll settings.

result.title: Efficient crafting path
result.description: Suggested steps from your base toward the modifiers you listed. Currency costs are compared in exalted orbs.

resultCard.empty: Press the button below to generate the recommended path for this base and your targets. With no targets, you will only see a reminder to add some.

efficientPlan.heading: Recommended order (exalt-efficient)
efficientPlan.intro: Orb of Alchemy is usually the cheapest way to reach rare. Then anchor each goal mod with a Greater Essence, and refine with Chaos Orbs.
efficientPlan.costsLine: Reference cost per use (exalted): alchemy ≈ {alchemy}, chaos ≈ {chaos}, sim-essence ≈ {essence}.
efficientPlan.rlNote: Reinforcement learning in this project scores policies that spend fewer exalts in this normalised sense.

steps.selectTargetsFirst: Add target modifiers in the modifiers panel. Without them we cannot tie essences and chaos steps to concrete goals.
steps.alchemyNormalMagic: On a normal or magic base, use Orb of Alchemy to make the item rare (2 prefixes and 2 suffixes rolled at random). This is the lowest-exalt entry to rare in our model.
steps.essenceEachDesired: For each of the {count} target modifiers, use a matching Greater Essence to force that line. (High exalt cost, but cuts variance.)
steps.chaosUntilTargets: In PoE2, Chaos removes one explicit then adds another. Repeat until the remaining goals appear.
steps.exaltTopUp: If prefix/suffix slots remain, fill them with Exalted Orbs.
steps.annulOptional: If only unwanted mods block progress, consider Orb of Annulment, then revisit chaos/exalt.
